_readme: Independently-verified truth for each label image, used to score OCR config quality. For each image: open the corresponding file in public/demo-labels/, read the label by eye, and correct any field below to match exactly what is printed (verbatim, including case/punctuation). Set null for fields that are genuinely not printed on that image (e.g. back labels often lack a government warning). Set "verified" to true once you've checked an entry against the actual image. Unverified entries were pre-filled from tests/mocks/labels/_extracted.json as a starting guess only — they are NOT trustworthy until you check them.

12345-imports.governmentWarning: GOVERNMENT WARNING: (1) According to the Surgeon General, women should not drink alcoholic beverages during pregnancy because of the risk of birth defects. (2) Consumption of alcoholic beverages impairs your ability to drive a car or operate machinery, and may cause health problems.
